issue_summary: Add methods to handle time zone differences
javadoc_before:
/** * Converts the instant value to a GregorianCalendar * adjusting to take into account the different time zone rules. *

* Note that this method may not be reversible if the zone rules differ * around a daylight savings, or other, change. * * @param instant the instant to convert * @return the offset to add to UTC * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the instant is null * @since 1.2 */
javadoc_after:
/** * Converts the instant value to a GregorianCalendar * adjusting to take into account the different time zone rules. *

* This method is the equivalent of creating a calendar object and * calling set using each field in turn. The result is a calendar that * reflects the fields of the instant, rather than millisecond value. *

* This method can 'lose' data depending on the date and zone rules. * If a datetime exists in the specified instant that cannot be * represented in a calendar (due to differences in the time zone rules) * the JDK chooses to move the hour backwards. For example, 1972-03-26 * 01:30 in Europe/London cannot be represented in Calendar * in JDK1.3 due to an incorrect time zone definition in the JDK. * Instead, the JDK sets the time to 00:30. * * @param instant the instant to convert * @return the offset to add to UTC * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the instant is null * @since 1.2 */
